summary: TIMESTAMP TIMEZONE FIX VERIFIED. All API endpoints now return timestamps with 'Z' UTC suffix. Frontend correctly interprets and displays local time. All backend tests (14/14) passed. UI verification confirmed timestamps in Audit Logs, Users list, User details, and Auth History match current browser time. No 1-hour offset observed.

bug_fix_verification.issue: Audit Logs timestamps appeared 1 hour behind actual computer time
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: MongoDB returns naive datetimes (no tzinfo). Python's .isoformat() produces strings without timezone suffix. JavaScript interprets these as LOCAL time instead of UTC.

fix_implemented.description: Adds 'Z' suffix to all timestamps so JavaScript correctly interprets them as UTC and converts to local time

observations: Recent login events match current time
created_at and last_login_at timestamps display correctly
Login Activity section shows timestamps like '2/23/2026, 9:57:13 AM'
Events sorted by time (most recent first)

regression_tests.transfers_queue: PASS - Shows submitted transfers with correct timestamps

critical_code_review_comments: format_timestamp_utc() helper correctly handles naive datetimes by assuming UTC and adding 'Z' suffix
All four affected endpoints updated consistently: audit-logs, admin/users, user details, auth-history
context_for_next_testing_agent: Timestamp timezone fix verified working. All timestamps now include 'Z' suffix for UTC. Frontend correctly converts to local time. All admin sections (Overview, Users, KYC Queue, Accounts, Card Requests, Transfers Queue, Support Tickets, Audit Logs) working correctly.
